channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sindh High Court directs CBC to ensure sustainable water supply in DHA within three months
item.description: The Sindh High Court (SHC) has directed the Cantonment Board Clifton (CBC) to establish a sustainable piped water supply system in Karachi’s Defence Housing Authority (DHA) within three months. This o...

item.title: U.S. Enforces Naval Blockade on Iran Amid Plans for New Peace Talks
item.description: The United States has implemented a naval blockade near the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to curb Iranian oil exports while both nations prepare for a second round of peace negotiations. More than a dozen ...

item.title: Aimee Canny breaks African record and qualifies twice for Commonwealth Games
item.description: At the South African swimming championships in Gqeberha, Aimee Canny made an impressive showing, setting a continental record and securing two Commonwealth Games qualifying times in under 30 minutes. ...

item.title: Loko Pasifiki considered for NRL selection after denied release request
item.description: St George Illawarra forward Loko Pasifiki Tonga may return to first-grade NRL this weekend despite his club denying a release request. The 20-year-old prop has expressed frustration over limited first...

item.title: LPG shortage continues to trouble hotel and restaurant sector in Ernakulam during peak season
item.description: The ongoing shortage of LPG cylinders is still causing serious disruption to hotel and restaurant operations in Ernakulam, even as the region enters the busy vacation season. Many eateries in the dist...

item.title: Canadian Players and Storylines to Watch in the 2026 NBA Playoffs
item.description: The 2026 NBA playoffs are underway, featuring several compelling Canadian storylines. Four Canadians are part of the play-in games: Dillon Brooks of the Phoenix Suns, Shaedon Sharpe of the Portland Tr...

item.title: Businessman faces asset preservation after fraudulent TERS claims
item.description: Nikluis Manuel, director of Nako Mang Trading Enterprise CC, has been implicated in a major misuse of the Temporary Employer/Employee Relief Scheme (TERS) funds. After fraudulently claiming R19,1 mill...

item.title: Supporting New Fathers: Addressing the Gap in Guidance and Resources
item.description: Zac Seidler reflects on the challenges of becoming a father and how contemporary dads often lack structured guidance and support. Unlike mothers, who typically receive detailed advice and resources fr...

item.title: ADC resolves leadership disputes and outlines plan for 2027 elections
item.description: Yesterday, for their national convention, the African Democratic Congress (ADC) expel some top members including former Deputy National Chairman Nafiu Bala and House of Representatives member Leke Abe...

item.title: Updated Women's Health Strategy Aims to Address Ongoing Failures in Care and Listening to Patients
item.description: The UK government has announced a new update to England’s women’s health strategy, first introduced in 2022, following continued criticism that women’s health concerns are frequently dismissed within ...

item.title: Lagos Coroner Court Sets May 5 For Inquest Into Death of Chimamanda Adichie’s Son
item.description: Naija News reports say the coroner’s court for Lagos don fix May 5 for proper hearing of the matter wey concern the death of Master Nkanu Adichie-Esege, the pikin of famous writer Chimamanda Adichie. ...

item.title: Wes Streeting announces NHS reforms to improve women’s healthcare and address systemic bias
item.description: UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting has unveiled a wide-ranging package of reforms aimed at tackling what he calls the National Health Service’s failure to properly care for women and its tolerance of ‘...

item.title: US-Iran Negotiations May Resume Amid Regional Tensions and Blockade
item.description: Diplomatic efforts between the United States and Iran are continuing as Vice President JD Vance may lead a new round of negotiations in Pakistan before the current ceasefire expires next week. Preside...

item.title: Study Shows Carbohydrate Preference Can Reduce Energy Expenditure and Promote Weight Gain
item.description: Researchers at Osaka Metropolitan University have found that carbohydrate-rich foods like bread, rice, and wheat can lead to weight gain in mice even without increasing calorie intake. In the study, m...

item.title: Reforming Political Parties for Stronger Democracy in Kenya
item.description: In Kenya, political parties are meant to be the main channels for electing leaders, but many have turned into vehicles for personal gain. The article points out that some individuals register 'briefca...

item.title: Kew’s Chowon Delivers High-Quality Japanese Dining at Affordable Prices
item.description: Chowon, a Japanese restaurant located in Melbourne's Kew suburb, has quickly become a standout for offering fine-dining experiences without the hefty price tag. Opened last year by Korean-Australian c...

item.title: TV Mohandas Pai Urges TCS To Investigate Conversion And Harassment Allegations At Nashik Branch
item.description: Business leader T.V. Mohandas Pai has urged Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) to take firm action after allegations surfaced involving an HR executive at its Nashik office who allegedly pressured employ...

item.title: U.S. Vice-President urges immigrants to prioritise American identity after naturalisation
item.description: In a recent address at the University of Georgia on April 14, 2026, U.S. Vice-President J.D. Vance emphasised that immigrants who become American citizens should prioritise their American identity ove...

item.title: IMF Revises Down Global Economic Growth for 2026 Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has revised down its forecast for global economic growth in 2026, citing the ongoing conflict in the Middle East as a major factor affecting international markets...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte Responds to Allegations on Law School Performance Amid Impeachment Proceedings
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte strongly defended her academic record amid impeachment complaints during a House committee on justice hearing, which she described as a 'mini trial.' Duterte rejected claim...
